summary: Full backend + frontend testing of Scribeverse (writing dictation app). Backend: 14 pytest tests covering auth (httpOnly cookies, register/login/me/logout), projects/characters/chapters/acts/notes CRUD, multi-tag note filtering, per-note + per-project TXT exports, and ownership isolation (user A vs user B) — all 14 PASSED (100%). Frontend: end-to-end UI flow on the public preview URL — registered new user, created project, created character/chapter/act, created manual note with multi-tag (character chip + chapter + act), filtered notes by character, edited note, exported single note (download trigger: note-First_Note.txt), exported whole project (download trigger: UI_Test_Project.txt), deleted note, opened Dictate page (record button + manual textarea fallback), saved dictation note, and verified logout clears cookies and redirects to /login.

issues: Heading copy 'Return to the page.' reads oddly — likely meant 'Return to the page' as a greeting but ambiguous.
User email in top-right gets truncated/overflows when long (observed with email 'ui_5e86c98b@example.com' overlapping the logout area).

action_items: (Minor) Tighten the header email overflow/truncation so long emails don't collide with the logout button.
(Nit) Consider rewording the login page hero copy 'Return to the page.' for clarity.

(Dev-mode warning, non-blocking) React hydration warning in console: '<span> cannot be a child of <option>' — caused by the Emergent dev instrumentation (x-* attributes injecting <span data-ve-dynamic> inside <option> elements in chapter/act <select>s in ProjectPage). Harmless in production but spams dev console.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py (~630 lines) is approaching the 700-line soft cap; consider splitting into routers (auth.py, projects.py, notes.py, exports.py) before it grows further.
Exports use proj['title'] directly in Content-Disposition filename; non-ASCII titles could break the header. Consider RFC5987 (filename*=) for safety.
CORS_ORIGINS env is set to '*' but code correctly filters it out and only uses FRONTEND_URL with allow_credentials=True, which is safe. Good.
Admin seed is idempotent only on existence — if ADMIN_PASSWORD changes in env it won't be updated. Consider upsert of the password hash when env changes (per playbook guideline).
No rate-limit / brute-force lockout on /auth/login (playbook recommends lockout after 5 failed attempts). Not critical for MVP but worth noting.
get_current_user: uses HTTPException 401 on user-not-found — good. JWT refresh_token is issued but never used by any endpoint (no /auth/refresh). Either remove or add refresh flow.
Pydantic EmailStr rejects .test TLD — be aware this will also reject internal/staging emails; acceptable for MVP.
Note export/project export don't sanitize filename beyond replacing spaces; unicode and control chars possible.
test_credentials: admin@scribeverse.app / admin123 (seeded admin) verified working; ui-test users registered dynamically via register form.
seed_data_creation: Backend tests create TEST_ prefixed users (test_a_*/test_b_* @example.com) and projects (TEST_*). UI test created one user 'ui_<uuid>@example.com' with project 'UI Test Project' containing a dictation note.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend regression suite is at /app/backend/tests/backend_test.py (14 tests, all green). Use pytest with --junitxml=/app/test_reports/pytest/pytest_results.xml. Use @example.com (NOT .test TLD) for registration payloads because Pydantic EmailStr rejects reserved TLDs. httpOnly cookies work correctly via requests.Session(). Admin login: admin@scribeverse.app / admin123. The login page hero copy 'Return to the page.' is real (not a typo to fix in tests).
rca of the issue: No critical issues. Only two minor UI polish items and some non-blocking code-review suggestions listed above.
